steps.title: Use the practical-value pivot
steps.content: The passage says youth underrepresentation is broadly viewed as not inherently unfair. Mensah therefore cannot be arguing for quotas because the underrepresentation is unfair in principle. The phrase "instrumental value for promoting youth interests" points to practical outcomes for young people.

steps.title: Reject fairness-based claims
steps.content: A says the experience concern is not relevant, but the passage calls it legitimate. B and D both claim youth underrepresentation is inherently unfair, contradicting the stated consensus. The remaining option accepts fairness in principle while arguing that better youth outcomes justify quotas. The answer is C.
